Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Canada Elections Act
Identification.BillSponsor: MINISTER OF PUBLIC SAFETY, DEMOCRATIC INSTITUTIONS AND INTERGOVERNMENTAL AFFAIRSâ

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Canada Elections Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: provide for two additional days of advance polling;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: authorize returning officers to constitute polling divisions that consist of a single institution, or part of an institution, where seniors or persons with a disability reside and provide for the procedures for voting at polling stations in those polling divisions;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: update the process for voting by special ballot;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: provide for the establishment of offices for voting by special ballot at post-secondary educational institutions;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: provide for new requirements relating to political partiesâ policies for the protection of personal information;

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: establish new prohibitions and modify existing prohibitions, including in relation to foreign influence in the electoral process, the provision of false or misleading information respecting elections and the acceptance or use of certain contributions; and

Provision.Label: (g)
Provision.Text: expand the scope of certain provisions relating to the administration and enforcement of that Act, including by granting the Commissioner of Canada Elections certain powers in respect of any conspiracy or attempt to commit, or being an accessory after the fact or counselling in relation to, a contravention of that Act.

The enactment also provides that the Chief Electoral Officer must make a report on the measures that need to be taken to implement a three-day polling period, a report on the measures that need to be taken to enable electors to vote at any place in their polling station, a report on the feasibility of enabling electors to vote at any polling station in their electoral district and a report proposing a process for the determination of whether a political party has as one of its fundamental purposes the promotion of hatred against an identifiable group of persons.
His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Electoral Participation Act.
The definition polling station in subsection 2(1) of the Canada Elections Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: polling stationâmeans a place established under section 120, 122, 124, 125, 205, 206, 207, 253 or 255 for electors to cast their votes.â(bureau de scrutin)
polling stationâmeans a place established under section 120, 122, 125, 205, 206, 207, 253 or 255 for electors to cast their votes.â(bureau de scrutin)

Section.Text: Subsections 18.1(2) to (4) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer may devise and test an alternative voting process for future use in one or more elections.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Voting technology â electors with a disability
Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer shall develop, obtain or adapt voting technology for use by electors with a disability and may test the technology for future use in one or more elections.

An alternative voting process or voting technology tested under subsection (2) or (3) may, with the prior approval of the committees of the Senate and of the House of Commons that normally consider electoral matters, be used in one or more elections held within six years after the day on which the approval is granted.

Each time an alternative voting process or voting technology approved for use under subsection (4) is to be used, the Chief Electoral Officer shall notify the committees of the election in which it is to be used and the way in which it is to be used.

The Chief Electoral Officer may make modifications in relation to an alternative voting process or voting technology approved for use under subsection (4) that are, in the Chief Electoral Officerâs opinion, minor technical or operational modifications.
The Chief Electoral Officer may devise and test an alternative voting process for future use in an election.
The Chief Electoral Officer shall develop, obtain or adapt voting technology for use by electors with a disability, and may test the technology for future use in an election.

Neither an alternative voting process nor voting technology tested under subsection (2) or (3) may be used in an election without the prior approval of the committees of the Senate and of the House of Commons that normally consider electoral matters.

Section.Text: Section 25 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Between the 1st and 20th days of January in each year, the Chief Electoral Officer shall publish a list in the Canada Gazette of the name, municipality (or equivalent) and province of residence and occupation of the returning officer for each electoral district in Canada.

Between the 1st and 20th days of January in each year, the Chief Electoral Officer shall publish a list in the Canada Gazette of the name, address and occupation of the returning officer for each electoral district in Canada.
Subsection 56.1(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (1), each general election must be held on the third Monday of October in the fourth calendar year following polling day for the last general election. However, if Monday, October 20, 2025 would be the day fixed for voting at a general election under this subsection, that general election must instead be held on Monday, October 27, 2025.

Subject to subsection (1), each general election must be held on the third Monday of October in the fourth calendar year following polling day for the last general election, with the first general election after this section comes into force being held on Monday, October 19, 2009.
Subsections 56.2(1) and (2) of the Act are replaced by the following:

If the Chief Electoral Officer is of the opinion that a Monday that would otherwise be polling day under subsection 56.1(2) is not suitable for that purpose, including by reason of its being in conflict with a day of cultural or religious significance or a provincial or municipal election, the Chief Electoral Officer may choose another day in accordance with subsection (4) and shall, no later than March 1 in the year before the year in which the general election is to be held, recommend to the Governor in Council that polling day be that other day and provide the Governor in Council with the reasons for the Chief Electoral Officerâs opinion and recommendation.
For greater certainty, the Chief Electoral Officer may carry out any consultations the Chief Electoral Officer considers appropriate before recommending that polling day be another day.

If the Chief Electoral Officer recommends an alternate day for a general election in accordance with subsection (1), the Chief Electoral Officer shall without delay publish in the Canada Gazette notice of the day recommended and the reasons provided under subsection (1).

If the Chief Electoral Officer is of the opinion that a Monday that would otherwise be polling day under subsection 56.1(2) is not suitable for that purpose, including by reason of its being in conflict with a day of cultural or religious significance or a provincial or municipal election, the Chief Electoral Officer may choose another day in accordance with subsection (4) and shall recommend to the Governor in Council that polling day be that other day.

If the Chief Electoral Officer recommends an alternate day for a general election in accordance with subsection (1), he or she shall without delay publish in the Canada Gazette notice of the day recommended.
Subsections 56.2(4) and (5) of the Act are replaced by the following:

The alternate day must be either the Tuesday immediately following the Monday that would otherwise be polling day or a Monday in the two weeks before or after the week in which the election would otherwise be held.
An order under subsection (3) shall not be made after September 1 in the year before the year in which the general election is to be held.

If the Governor in Council rejects the recommendation, the Chief Electoral Officer shall exercise any of the Chief Electoral Officerâs powers under this Act that the Chief Electoral Officer considers necessary for the purpose of enabling electors to exercise their right to vote.
The alternate day must be either the Tuesday immediately following the Monday that would otherwise be polling day or the Monday of the following week.
An order under subsection (3) shall not be made after August 1 in the year in which the general election is to be held.
Paragraphs 66(1)(e) to (g) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: for any electoral district except one listed in Schedule 3, the names, addresses and signatures of at least 75 electors resident in the electoral district;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: for an electoral district listed in Schedule 3, the names, addresses and signatures of at least 50 electors resident in the electoral district; and

Subsection.Text: A nomination paper shall be in the prescribed form and include

Paragraph.Text: Â

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: for any electoral district except one listed in Schedule 3, the names, addresses and signatures, made in the presence of a witness, of at least 100 electors resident in the electoral district;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: for an electoral district listed in Schedule 3, the names, addresses and signatures, made in the presence of a witness, of at least 50 electors resident in the electoral district;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the name, address and signature of the witness to each signature made under paragraph (e) or (f); and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 66(4) of the Act is repealed.

A witness to a signature referred to in paragraph (1)(e) or (f) shall use due diligence to ensure that the signatures that are made in his or her presence are all made by electors resident in the electoral district.

Section.Text: Subsection 67(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A prospective candidate shall file the nomination paper with the returning officer in the electoral district in which the prospective candidate is seeking nomination at any time during the period that begins on one of the following days and ends on the closing day for nominations:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the first day of the pre-election period in the case of a general election that is held on a day set in accordance with subsection 56.1(2) or section 56.2; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the date of the issue of the Notice of Election in any other case.

Subsection.Text: A prospective candidate shall file the nomination paper with the returning officer in the electoral district in which the prospective candidate is seeking nomination at any time between the date of the issue of the Notice of Election and the close of nominations.

Section.Text: Section 69 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The closing day for nominations shall be Saturday, the 23rd day before polling day.
The closing day for nominations shall be Monday, the 21st day before polling day.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 71(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Notice to candidate of confirmation or refusal

Subsection.Text: The returning officer shall, not later than 96 hours after a nomination paper is filed, give the prospective candidate notice, in the prescribed form, of the confirmation of the nomination or of the refusal to accept the nomination.

The returning officer shall, not later than 48 hours after a nomination paper is filed, give the prospective candidate notice, in the prescribed form, of the confirmation of the nomination or of the refusal to accept the nomination.
Section 71 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Confirmation of nomination â pre-election period
Subsection.Text: A confirmation of a nomination that is given during the pre-election period does not have effect until the date of the issue of the Notice of Election.

Section.Text: Subsection 71.1(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Notice of name to appear on ballot
Subsection.Text: The returning officer shall, not later than 96 hours after the nomination paper is filed, give the prospective candidate notice, in the prescribed form, of whether the name referred to in subparagraph 66(1)(a)(i.1) is the name that is to appear on the ballot in respect of the prospective candidate.

The returning officer shall, not later than 48 hours after the nomination paper is filed, give the prospective candidate notice, in the prescribed form, of whether the name referred to inCla subparagraph 66(1)(a)(i.1) is the name that is to appear on the ballot in respect of the prospective candidate.

Section.Text: Subsection 91(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies regardless of the place where the election is held or the place where the false statement is made or published and regardless of the manner or medium in which the false statement is made or published.
Subsection (1) applies regardless of the place where the election is held or the place where the false statement is made or published.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 92:

Section.MarginalNote: Conveying false or misleading information â nomination paper
Section.Text: No person or entity shall convey or cause to be conveyed false or misleading information to be included in a nomination paper.

Section.MarginalNote: Filing nomination paper â false or misleading information
Section.Text: No person who files a nomination paper under section 67 shall file a nomination paper containing false or misleading information.
Subsection 93(1.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer shall make available in electronic form or in formats that include electronic form, to each registered party or eligible party that requests them, the preliminary lists of electors for an electoral district in respect of which a writ has been issued if the party

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: was represented in the House of Commons on the day before the day on which the writ was issued;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: endorsed a candidate in one of the last two elections in that electoral district; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: endorsed candidates in at least two thirds of all electoral districts in the last general election.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of paragraph (1.1)(b), if the boundaries of an electoral district are revised or a new electoral district is established as a result of a representation order referred to in section 25 of the Electoral Boundaries Readjustment Act, a candidate who, in one of those last two elections, was endorsed in an electoral district that coincides with all or part of the revised electoral district or new electoral district is considered to have been endorsed in the revised or new electoral district.

The Chief Electoral Officer shall make available in electronic form or in formats that include electronic form, to each registered party or eligible party that requests them, the preliminary lists of electors for an electoral district in respect of which a writ has been issued.
The portion of subsection 95(2) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:
Subject to subsection (2.1), the notice of confirmation of registration shall be in the form established by the Chief Electoral Officer and shall indicate
The notice of confirmation of registration shall be in the form established by the Chief Electoral Officer and shall indicate
Section 95 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Contents of notice â polling stations in institutions
Subsection.Text: In the case of an elector whose polling station is established under subsection 124(1), the notice of confirmation of registration shall be in the form established by the Chief Electoral Officer and shall indicate

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the address of the electorâs polling station, and whether or not it is in premises that are accessible to electors with a disability;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the dates on which and hours during which the polling station is open;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the fact that proof of the electorâs identity will be required before the elector is allowed to vote at the electorâs polling station;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the dates of advance polls and the voting hours and locations of advance polling stations;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the fact that proof of the electorâs identity and residence will be required before the elector is allowed to vote at an advance polling station; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a telephone number to call for more information.

Section.Text: Subsection 96(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Revision of preliminary lists of electors

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer shall, as soon as possible after the issue of a writ, fix the commencement date for the revision of the preliminary lists of electors. The revision period shall end at 6:00 p.m. on the 5th day before polling day.

The Chief Electoral Officer shall, as soon as possible after the issue of a writ, fix the commencement date for the revision of the preliminary lists of electors. The revision period shall terminate at 6:00 p.m. on the 6th day before polling day.

Section.Text: Section 102 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Each returning officer shall, as early as possible during the revision period but not later than the 5th day before polling day, send a notice of confirmation of registration that contains the information described in subsection 95(2) or (2.1), as the case may be, and subsection 95(3) to every elector whose name has been added to a preliminary list of electors during the revision period, except electors referred to in subsection 95(1).

Each returning officer shall, as early as possible during the revision period but not later than the 5th day before polling day, send a notice of confirmation of registration that contains the information described in subsections 95(2) and (3) to every elector whose name has been added to a preliminary list of electors during the revision period, except electors referred to in subsection 95(1).
Sections 105 and 106 of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Each returning officer shall, on the 12th day before polling day, prepare a revised list of electors for each polling division in the electoral district for use at the advance poll.

The Chief Electoral Officer shall, not later than the 6th day before polling day, determine the number of names appearing on the revised lists of electors for each electoral district and cause that information to be published in the Canada Gazette.

Section.Text: Each returning officer shall, without delay after the 6th day before polling day but no later than the 3rd day before polling day, prepare the official list of electors for each polling station for use on polling day.

Subsection.Text: Each returning officer shall, on the 11th day before polling day, prepare a revised list of electors for each polling division in the electoral district for use at the advance poll.

The Chief Electoral Officer shall, not later than the 7th day before polling day, determine the number of names appearing on the revised lists of electors for each electoral district and cause that information to be published in the Canada Gazette.

Section.Text: Each returning officer shall, without delay after the 7th day before polling day but no later than the 3rd day before polling day, prepare the official list of electors for each polling station for use on polling day.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 109:

Subsection.Text: At the written request of an elector, the Chief Electoral Officer shall exclude, for a period of five years, the electorâs name and address, as well as the identifier assigned to them by the Chief Electoral Officer, from the lists made available to registered parties, eligible parties, members or candidates under subsection 45(1) or 93(1.1), section 94, 104.1 or 104.2 or subsection 107(3) or (4) or 109(2).
The Chief Electoral Officer shall publish each year, in a manner that the Chief Electoral Officer considers appropriate, the number of requests received the preceding year under subsection (1).

Section.Text: Paragraphs 119(1)(g) and (h) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a ballot box for polling day and one or more ballot boxes for advance polling;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the text of the solemn declarations to be made by electors; and

Subsection.Text: Before voting begins, each returning officer shall provide, in accordance with the Chief Electoral Officerâs instructions, the election officers who are assigned to a polling station in the returning officerâs electoral district with

Paragraph.Text: Â

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a ballot box for polling day and a separate ballot box for each day of advance polling;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the text of the oaths to be administered to electors; and

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following before section 125:

Section.MarginalNote: Polling station consisting of long-term care institution

Subsection.Text: If a polling division consisting of one institution or a part of an institution is constituted under subsection 538(5), the returning officer shall establish a polling station in the institution or part of the institution.

The returning officer shall open a polling station established under subsection (1) for the days and hours â during the period that begins on the 13th day before polling day and ends on polling day â that the returning officer considers necessary to give electors residing in the institution or part of the institution, as the case may be, a reasonable opportunity to vote, but the polling station is not to be open for a total of more than 12 hours during that period.

The returning officer shall give notice to the candidates of the days and hours that a polling station established under subsection (1) is open in accordance with the instructions of the Chief Electoral Officer.

Subject to section 125.01 and the instructions of the Chief Electoral Officer, the provisions of this Act that relate to polling stations shall, in so far as they are applicable, apply to a polling station established under subsection (1).

Section.Text: Subsection 125(4) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subject to section 125.01 and the instructions of the Chief Electoral Officer, the provisions of this Act that relate to polling stations shall, in so far as they are applicable, apply to mobile polling stations.
Subject to the instructions of the Chief Electoral Officer, the provisions of this Act that relate to ordinary polls shall, in so far as they are applicable, apply to mobile polling stations.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 125:

Subject to the instructions of the Chief Electoral Officer, if an elector whose place of ordinary residence is in an institution or part of an institution in which a polling station is established under subsection 124(1) or 125(1) wishes to vote at the polling station, vouch for another elector at the polling station or register their name on the list of electors in person at the polling station, the following rules apply:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the elector is not required to provide proof of residence for the purposes of subsection 143(2) or paragraph 161(1)(a) or prove residence for the purposes of subsection 143(3), section 144, subsection 147(1), section 148 or paragraph 161(1)(b);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of paragraphs 143(2)(a) and (b) and (3)(a) and 161(1)(a) and subparagraph 161(1)(b)(i), the elector is not required to provide pieces of identification that contain the electorâs address, establish the electorâs address or contain an address that proves the electorâs residence or is consistent with information related to the elector that appears on the list of electors;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the elector makes the solemn declaration referred to in subsection 549.1(1) for the purposes of subsection 143(3), section 144, subsection 147(1), section 148 or paragraph 161(1)(b), the solemn declaration is not required to include the statement referred to in paragraph 549.1(1)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if the elector makes the solemn declaration referred to in subsection 549.1(2) for the purposes of paragraph 143(3)(b) or subparagraph 161(1)(b)(ii), the solemn declaration is not required to include the statement referred to in paragraph 549.1(2)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of subsections 143(3.01), 143.1(1), 161(2) and 161.1(1), the elector may decide to prove identity only;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of subsection 143(4), the election officer is deemed to be satisfied that the electorâs residence has been proven;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: if the elector makes the solemn declaration referred to in section 146 or the solemn declaration referred to in the subsection 161(4) for the purposes of that section or subsection, that solemn declaration is not required to include the statement that the elector resides at the address at which the elector claims to reside; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of subsection 148.1(1), the elector is deemed to have proven residence in accordance with section 143.

Section.Text: Paragraph 127(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in person at a polling station on polling day or at a polling station established under subsection 124(1);

Section.Text: An elector may vote

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in person at a polling station on polling day;

Section.Text: Paragraph 135(1)(e) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: an elector and a person who is assisting the elector by virtue of subsection 155(1), only for the period necessary to enable the elector to vote;

Subsection.Text: The only persons who may be present at a polling station on polling day are

Paragraph.Text: Â

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: an elector and a friend or relative who is helping him or her by virtue of subsection 155(1), only for the period necessary to enable the elector to vote;

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 140:

Section.MarginalNote: Opening of polling station in long-term care institution

Subsection.Text: When a polling station established under subsection 124(1) opens, an election officer who is assigned to the polling station shall, in full view of the candidates or their representatives who are present,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on the first day that the polling station is open,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: open the ballot box and ascertain that it is empty,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: seal the ballot box with the seals provided by the Chief Electoral Officer, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: place the ballot box on a table in full view of all present and, subject to section 157, ensure that the box remains there until the polling station closes on that day; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on each subsequent day, if any, that the polling station is open, place the ballot box on a table in full view of all present and, subject to section 157, ensure that the box remains there until the polling station closes on that day.

Subsection.Text: At the close of the polling station on each day that it is open, an election officer who is assigned to the polling station shall, in full view of the candidates or their representatives who are present, and in accordance with instructions that the Chief Electoral Officer considers necessary to ensure the integrity of the vote, take the measures set out in those instructions.

If an election officer who is assigned to the polling station determines, in accordance with the Chief Electoral Officerâs instructions, that another ballot box is needed at the polling station, then an election officer who is assigned to the polling station shall, in full view of the candidates or their representatives who are present and in accordance with those instructions,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: take the steps set out in subparagraphs (1)(a)(i) to (iii) with regard to that other ballot box; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: take the steps set out in paragraph (1)(b) and in subsection (2) with regard to the ballot boxes in the circumstances set out in those instructions.

Subsection.Text: The candidates or their representatives may take note of the serial numbers of the seals on the following ballot boxes at the times indicated:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for any ballot box that was used at the polling station, note may be taken when the polling station closes;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for a ballot box that is placed on the table in accordance with subsection (1) or (3), note may be taken when it is placed there; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for each of the ballot boxes that were used at the polling station, note may be taken when the votes are counted on polling day.

Subsection.Text: Until the counting of the ballots on polling day, an election officer shall keep the sealed ballot box or boxes in their custody in accordance with the Chief Electoral Officerâs instructions.
Subsections 175(6) to (9) apply in respect of each ballot box used at the polling station.
Subsection 155(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
If an elector requires assistance to vote, a person may accompany the elector into the voting compartment and assist the elector to mark their ballot.
If an elector requires assistance to vote, one of the following persons may accompany the elector into the voting compartment and assist the elector to mark his or her ballot:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a friend of the elector;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the electorâs spouse or common-law partner; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a relative of the elector or of the electorâs spouse or common-law partner.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 155(3) of the Act is amended by adding âandâ at the end of paragraph (b), by striking out âandâ at the end of paragraph (c) and by repealing paragraph (d).
A person described in subsection (1) who wishes to assist an elector in marking a ballot shall first make a solemn declaration, in the prescribed form, that he or she

Paragraph.Text: Â

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: has not, during the current election, assisted another person, as a friend, to mark a ballot.

Section.Text: Section 158 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Transfer certificate for elector in certain circumstances
Subsection.Text: An elector whose name appears on the list of electors for a polling station and who, on polling day, attends at another polling station in the same electoral district is entitled on request to receive a transfer certificate to vote at that other polling station if they make a solemn declaration, in the prescribed form, that they are unable to attend at the polling station for which their name appears on the list of electors before its close.

Section.Text: Subsection 168(8) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Advance polling stations in one or more communities
Subsection.Text: If the returning officer is of the opinion that an advance polling district consists of or includes remote, isolated or low-density communities, the returning officer may, with the prior approval of the Chief Electoral Officer and in accordance with the Chief Electoral Officerâs instructions, establish the advance polling station for that district, in premises in one or more of those communities, that shall be open for the days and hours, during the period that begins on the 11th day before polling day and ends on the 6th day before polling day, that the returning officer considers necessary to give electors who reside in the district a reasonable opportunity to vote. For greater certainty, subsections (5) to (7) apply to the advance polling station.

If the returning officer is of the opinion that an advance polling district consists of or includes remote, isolated or low-density communities, the returning officer may, with the prior approval of the Chief Electoral Officer and in accordance with the Chief Electoral Officerâs instructions, establish the advance polling station for that district in premises in more than one such community, and have the election officers who are assigned to the advance polling station â along with the ballot box, ballots and other necessary election documents â be at given ones of those premises on different days of advance polling to take electorsâ votes. For greater certainty, subsections (5) to (7) apply to the advance polling station.

Section.Text: Section 168.1 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Transfer certificate for elector in certain circumstances
Subsection.Text: An elector whose name appears on the revised list of electors for an advance polling station established under subsection 168(8) and who attends at another advance polling station in the same electoral district is entitled on request to receive a transfer certificate to vote at that other polling station if they make a solemn declaration, in the prescribed form, that they are unable to attend at the advance polling station for which their name appears on the revised list of electors before its close.

Section.Text: Subsection 171(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: When advance polling stations to be open
Subsection.Text: An advance polling station shall be open fromÂ 9:00 a.m. to 9:00Â p.m. on Thursday, Friday, Saturday, Sunday, Monday and Tuesday, the 11th, 10th, 9th, 8th, 7th and 6th days, respectively, before polling day, and shall not be open at any other time.

An advance polling station shall be open from 9:00Â a.m. to 9:00Â p.m. on Friday, Saturday, Sunday and Monday, the 10th, 9th, 8th and 7th days, respectively, before polling day, and shall not be open at any other time.
Paragraph 175(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on the 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 5th and 6th days of advance polling, place the ballot box on a table in full view of all present and keep it there until the close of the advance polling station on that day.

Subsection.Text: At the opening of an advance polling station at 9:00 a.m., an election officer who is assigned to the advance polling station shall, in full view of the candidates or their representatives who are present,

Paragraph.Text: Â

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on the 2nd, 3rd and 4th days of advance polling, place the ballot box on a table in full view of all present and keep it there until the close of the advance polling station on that day.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 175(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

At the close of an advance polling station at 9:00 p.m. on each of the six days of advance polling, an election officer who is assigned to the advance polling station shall, in full view of the candidates or their representatives who are present, and in accordance with instructions that the Chief Electoral Officer considers necessary to ensure the integrity of the vote, take the measures set out in those instructions.

At the close of an advance polling station at 9:00 p.m. on each of the four days of advance polling, an election officer who is assigned to the advance polling station shall, in full view of the candidates or their representatives who are present, and in accordance with instructions that the Chief Electoral Officer considers necessary to ensure the integrity of the vote, take the measures set out in those instructions.
Paragraph 175(4)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for any ballot box that was used on a day of advance polling, note may be taken when an advance polling station closes on each of the six days of advance polling;

Subsection.Text: The candidates or their representatives may take note of the serial numbers of the seals on the following ballot boxes at the times indicated:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for any ballot box that was used on a day of advance polling, note may be taken when an advance polling station closes on each of the four days of advance polling;

Subsection.Text: Subsection 175(7) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Subsection 176(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Collecting the record of votes cast at an advance polling station

Subsection.Text: As soon as possible after the close of advance polling stations on Tuesday, the 6th day before polling day, the returning officer shall have the original copy of the record of votes cast collected from the advance polling stations.

As soon as possible after the close of advance polling stations on Monday, the 7th day before polling day, the returning officer shall have the original copy of the record of votes cast collected from the advance polling stations.

Section.Text: Section 224 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The address indicated as the place of ordinary residence in Canada in the application for registration and special ballot cannot be changed after the electorâs name is entered in the register.
The address chosen as the place of ordinary residence in Canada in the application for registration and special ballot cannot be changed after the electorâs name is entered in the register.
Subsection 232(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Conditions for voting by special ballot

Subsection.Text: An elector may vote under this Division if the electorâs application for registration and special ballot, which may be submitted in writing or in electronic form, is received by a returning officer in an electoral district or by the special voting rules administrator during the pre-election period or after the issue of the writs but before 6:00Â p.m. on the 6th day before polling day.

An elector may vote under this Division if his or her application for registration and special ballot is received by a returning officer in an electoral district or by the special voting rules administrator after the issue of the writs but before 6:00Â p.m. on the 6th day before polling day.

Section.Text: Section 235 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Once an electorâs application for registration and special ballot has been accepted, the elector may vote only under this Division unless the elector

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: is authorized by the returning officer to vote other than under this Division;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: returns the special ballot and outer envelope that the elector received to a designated election officer in person at

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the office of the returning officer in the electorâs electoral district,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the electorâs advance polling station, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the electorâs polling station; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: makes the solemn declaration referred to in subsection 549.1(1) in writing.

Section.Text: Once an electorâs application for registration and special ballot has been accepted, the elector may only vote under this Division.
Subsection 239(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Receipt of special ballot â application made in electoral district
Subsection.Text: In order to have their special ballot counted, an elector whose application for registration and special ballot was accepted by the returning officer in the electorâs electoral district shall ensure

Paragraph.Text: that the ballot is received at the office of that returning officer before the close of the polling stations on polling day by sending the sealed outer envelope to the returning officer by mail or any other means; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that the ballot is received at a polling station in that district before the closing of the polling stations on polling day by depositing the sealed outer envelope in a ballot box for the deposit of outer envelopes installed under subsection (2.1).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Installation of ballot boxes for special ballots
Subsection.Text: The returning officer shall install a ballot box for the deposit of outer envelopes during voting hours on polling day in each polling station in the electoral district except polling stations within locations specified by the Chief Electoral Officer.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Locations without ballot boxes for special ballots
Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer shall, not later than the 6th day before polling day, publish a list of locations within which a ballot box for the deposit of outer envelopes is not to be installed at polling stations on the Chief Electoral Officerâs Internet site and cause that list to be published in the Canada Gazette.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Ballot boxes at close of polling station
Subsection.Text: At the close of a polling station, an election officer who is assigned to the polling station shall, in full view of the candidates or their representatives who are present, and in accordance with instructions that the Chief Electoral Officer considers necessary to ensure the integrity of the vote, take the measures set out in those instructions in respect of the outer envelopes deposited in ballot boxes.

In order to have their special ballot counted, an elector whose application for registration and special ballot was accepted by the returning officer in the electorâs electoral district shall ensure that the ballot is received at the office of that returning officer before the close of the polling stations on polling day.
Subsection 243.01(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:
If an elector requires assistance to vote, a person may accompany the elector into the voting compartment at the office of the returning officer and assist the elector to mark their ballot.

If an elector requires assistance to vote, one of the following persons may accompany the elector into the voting compartment at the office of the returning officer and assist the elector to mark his or her ballot:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a friend of the elector;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the electorâs spouse or common-law partner; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a relative of the elector or of the electorâs spouse or common-law partner.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 243.01(2) of the Act is amended by adding âandâ at the end of paragraph (b), by striking out âandâ at the end of paragraph (c) and by repealing paragraph (d).
A person described in subsection (1) who wishes to assist an elector in marking a ballot shall first make a solemn declaration in the prescribed form that he or she

Paragraph.Text: Â

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: has not, during the current election, assisted another person, as a friend, to mark a ballot.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 243.1:

Subsection.Text: The Chief Electoral Officer shall notify the post-secondary educational institutions that the Chief Electoral Officer considers appropriate that offices for voting by special ballot may be established on their premises in a general election.

A returning officer shall, with the prior approval of the Chief Electoral Officer and in accordance with the Chief Electoral Officerâs instructions, establish an office for voting by special ballot on the premises of a post-secondary educational institution in the returning officerâs electoral district if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an office for voting by special ballot was established on the institutionâs premises for the general election held in 2019 and the institution agrees to the establishment of an office for voting by special ballot on its premises; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the institution requests the returning officer to establish an office for voting by special ballot on its premises.

Subsection.Text: An elector who applies in person to vote at an office established under subsection (2) shall be given a special ballot and may vote by following the procedure set out in subsections 227(2) and (3) and return the outer envelope to an election officer at any such office.

An elector who has received a special ballot, other than an elector referred to in subsection (3), may vote in person at an office established under subsection (2) by following the procedure for voting set out in subsections 227(2) and (3) and return the outer envelope to an election officer at that office.

An office established under subsection (2) may be open during the period that begins on the 15th day before polling day and ends at 6:00 p.m. on the 8th day before polling day, but the office is not to be open for more than 12 hours each day that it is open during that period.

Section.Text: Section 269 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: No special ballot shall be rejected for the sole reason that the elector has written, instead of the name of a candidate, the name of a registered party that has endorsed a candidate in the electorâs electoral district, if the ballot clearly indicates the electorâs intent. The ballot is deemed to have the name of the candidate endorsed by that party written on it.

Section.Text: Section 279 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (3):

Subsection.Text: No special ballot shall be rejected for the sole reason that the elector has written, instead of the name of a candidate, the name of a registered party that has endorsed a candidate in the electorâs electoral district, if the ballot clearly indicates the electorâs intent. The ballot is deemed to have the name of the candidate endorsed by that party written on it.
Paragraph 281.7(1)(f) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: alter, deface or destroy a ballot, a special ballot, the initials of the election officer that are signed on a ballot or the number of the polling division or advance polling district that is marked on a ballot;

Subsection.Text: No person shall

Paragraph.Text: Â

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: alter, deface or destroy a ballot, the initials of the election officer that are signed on a ballot or the number of the polling division or advance polling district that is marked on a ballot;
Â

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: destroy, take, open or otherwise interfere with a ballot box or book or packet of ballots or special ballots otherwise than as provided by this Act or by instructions of the Chief Electoral Officer.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 281.7(1)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: destroy, take, open or otherwise interfere with a ballot box, inner or outer envelope or book or packet of ballots or special ballots otherwise than as provided by this Act or by instructions of the Chief Electoral Officer.

Section.Text: Subsection 282(1) of the Act is repealed.

Subsection.Text: At an election, no person shall, as a friend, assist under section 155 or 243.01, more than one elector for the purpose of marking a ballot.
The portion of subsection 282.4(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

No person or entity referred to in any of paragraphs (a) to (e) shall unduly influence an elector to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate, potential candidate, registered party or eligible party, at an election:

No person or entity referred to in any of paragraphs (a) to (e) shall, during an election period, unduly influence an elector to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate or registered party, at the election:

Paragraph.Text: Â

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation or entity incorporated, formed or otherwise organized outside Canada that does not carry on business in Canada or whose primary purpose in Canada during an election period is to influence electors during that period to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate or registered party, at the election;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 282.4(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation or entity incorporated, formed or otherwise organized outside Canada if it does not carry on business in Canada or one of the primary activities that it carries on in Canada consists of doing anything to influence electors to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate, potential candidate, registered party or eligible party, at an election;

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 282.4(2) of the Act before paragraph (b) is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of subsection (1), a person or entity unduly influences an elector to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate, potential candidate, registered party or eligible party, at an election if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: they knowingly incur any expense to directly promote or oppose a candidate or potential candidate in that election, a registered party or eligible party that has endorsed a candidate or potential candidate in that election or the leader of such a registered party or eligible party; or

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsection (1), a person or entity unduly influences an elector to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate or registered party, at an election if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: they knowingly incur any expense to directly promote or oppose a candidate in that election, a registered party that has endorsed a candidate in that election or the leader of such a registered party;

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 282.4(3) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

For greater certainty, subsection (1) does not apply if the only thing done by the person or entity to influence the elector to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for the particular candidate, potential candidate, registered party or eligible party, consists of

For greater certainty, subsection (1) does not apply if the only thing done by the person or entity to influence the elector to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for the particular candidate or registered party, consists of

Paragraph.Text: Â

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a statement by them that encourages the elector to vote or refrain from voting for any candidate or registered party in the election; or

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 282.4(3)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a statement by them that encourages the elector to vote or refrain from voting for any candidate, potential candidate, registered party or eligible party in the election; or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 282.4(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

No person or entity shall sell any advertising space to a person or entity to whom subsection (1) applies for the purpose of enabling that person or entity to transmit an election advertising message or partisan advertising message or to cause an election advertising message or partisan advertising message to be transmitted.

No person or entity shall sell any advertising space to a person or entity to whom subsection (1) applies for the purpose of enabling that person or entity to transmit an election advertising message or to cause an election advertising message to be transmitted.

Section.Text: Paragraph 284(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that has not been supplied by an election officer;

Subsection.Text: In examining the ballots, the election officer who counts the votes shall reject one

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that has not been supplied by him or her;

Section.Text: Subsection 330(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Prohibition â use of broadcasting station outside Canada

Subsection.Text: No person shall, with intent to influence persons to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate, potential candidate, registered party or eligible party, at an election, use a broadcasting station outside Canada, or aid, abet, counsel or procure the use of a broadcasting station outside Canada, for the broadcasting of any matter having reference to an election.

No person shall, with intent to influence persons to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate or registered party, at an election, use a broadcasting station outside Canada, or aid, abet, counsel or procure the use of a broadcasting station outside Canada, during an election period, for the broadcasting of any matter having reference to an election.

Section.Text: The portion of paragraph (a) of the definition third party in section 349 of the Act before subparagraph (i) is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in Divisions 0.1 and 0.2, a person or a group other than

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in Division 0.1, a person or a group other than

Section.Text: Paragraph (b) of the definition foreign entity in subsection 349.01(1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation or entity incorporated, formed or otherwise organized outside Canada if it does not carry on business in Canada or one of the primary activities that it carries on in Canada consists of doing anything to influence electors to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate, potential candidate, registered party or eligible party, at an election;
Â

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a corporation or entity incorporated, formed or otherwise organized outside Canada that does not carry on business in Canada or whose only activity carried on in Canada consists of doing anything to influence electors to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate or registered party, at an election;

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 349.03:

Section.MarginalNote: Prohibition â cryptoasset, money order or payment product
Section.Text: No third party shall accept a contribution made for partisan activity, partisan advertising, election advertising or election survey purposes that is in the form of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a cryptoasset, namely a digital asset protected by cryptographic measures;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a money order; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a prepaid payment product, namely a physical or electronic product that is or can be loaded with funds and that can be used to make withdrawals or purchase goods or services.

Section.MarginalNote: Return of cryptoasset, money order or payment product
Section.Text: If a third party receives a contribution referred to in section 349.04, the third party shall, within 30 days after becoming aware of the receipt of the contribution, take one of the following measures:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: return the contribution unused to the contributor;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if it is not possible to return the contribution, destroy it unused;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if it is not possible to return or destroy the contribution, convert it into money and pay the amount of it to the Chief Electoral Officer, who shall forward that amount to the Receiver General.

Section.Text: Subparagraph 349.4(2)(b)(i) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: it does not carry on business in Canada or one of the primary activities that it carries on in Canada during a pre-election period consists of doing anything to influence electors during that period to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate, potential candidate, registered party or eligible party, at the following election, and

Subsection.Text: In subsection (1), a foreign third party is a third party in respect of which

Paragraph.Text: if the third party is a corporation or entity,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: it does not carry on business in Canada, or its only activity carried on in Canada during a pre-election period consists of doing anything to influence electors during that period to vote or refrain from voting, or to vote or refrain from voting for a particular candidate or registered party, at the following election, and

Section.Text: The portion of subsection 349.6(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A third party shall register immediately after having incurred the following expenses in an aggregate amount of $1,500:
A third party shall register immediately after having incurred the following expenses in an aggregate amount of $500:
Section 349.91 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

If subsection 349.95(1) applies to the third party, the interim third-party expenses return shall include the following for each Canadian individual whose contributions of a total amount of more than $200 were used to pay for the regulated expenses under that subsection:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: their name and address;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of each contribution;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a list of the property or services that was contributed; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the date each contribution was made.

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 349.91(4)(a) to (c) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of contributions for partisan activity, partisan advertising, election advertising or election survey purposes that were received during the period referred to in paragraph (1)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for each contributor who made contributions of a total amount of more than $200 for partisan activity, partisan advertising, election advertising or election survey purposes during the period referred to in paragraph (1)(a), their name and address and the amount and date of each contribution; and

Subsection.Text: The interim third-party expenses return shall include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount, by class of contributor, of contributions for partisan activity, partisan advertising, election advertising or election survey purposes that were received during the period referred to in paragraph (1)(a);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subject to paragraph (c), for each contributor who made contributions of a total amount of more than $200 for partisan activity, partisan advertising, election advertising or election survey purposes during the period referred to in paragraph (1)(a), their name, address and class, and the amount and date of each contribution;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a numbered company that is a contributor referred to in paragraph (b), the name of the chief executive officer or president of that company; and

Subsection.Text: Subsection 349.91(7) of the Act is repealed.
For the purposes of paragraphs (4)(a) and (b), the following are the classes of contributor:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: individuals;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: businesses;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: commercial organizations;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: governments;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: trade unions;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: corporations without share capital other than trade unions; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: unincorporated organizations or associations other than trade unions.

Section.Text: The portion of section 349.94 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Prohibition â use of anonymous contributions
Section.Text: No third party shall use a contribution made for partisan activity, partisan advertising or election survey purposes for the purpose of any of the following if the third party does not know the name and address of the contributor:

No third party shall use a contribution made for partisan activity, partisan advertising or election survey purposes for the purpose of any of the following if the third party does not know the name and address of the contributor or is otherwise unable to determine within which class of contributor referred to in subsection 349.91(7) the contributor falls:
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 349.94:

Subsection.Text: A third party shall use only contributions from Canadian individuals to pay for regulated expenses and use, as regulated expenses, only property or services that are contributions from Canadian individuals.

However, if the total amount of contributions received, from all sources and for any purpose, by the third party during the previous year is equal to or less than 10% of its revenue for that year, the third party may use its own funds, namely funds that are not contributions made to it, to pay for regulated expenses and use, as regulated expenses, property or services that it has provided. In that case, the third party shall include financial statements for the previous year in the third-party expenses return referred to in section 359.

For the purposes of subsection (2), grants and contributions received from the Government of Canada or of a province or from a municipality are not included in determining the third partyâs revenue for the previous year.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Previous year â choice of third party
Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsections (2) and (3), the third party may choose as their previous year either

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the calendar year that precedes the calendar year in which a pre-election period occurs, or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the fiscal year that precedes the fiscal year in which a pre-election period occurs.

Subsection.Text: This section does not apply in respect of a third party who is an individual or a third party that is not required to register under subsection 349.6(1).
The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: Canadian individualâmeans an individual who is a Canadian citizen or a permanent resident as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Immigration and Refugee Protection Act.â(particulier canadien)
regulated expensesâmeans any of the following expenses:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: partisan activity expenses in relation to partisan activities that are carried out during a pre-election period;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: partisan advertising expenses in relation to partisan advertising messages that are transmitted during that period; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: election survey expenses in relation to election surveys that are conducted during that period.â(dÃ©penses rÃ©glementÃ©es)
